channel.title: Milanjovanovic.tech on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Milanjovanovic.tech on .NET Ramblings

item.title: When Your Use Case Half-Succeeds: Designing for Partial Failure in .NET
item.description: A use case isn't a transaction. The moment it touches more than one system, you are dealing with partial failure. Here's how I classify side effects and design use cases that fail loudly and recover safely. Link to article: https://www.milanjovanovic.tech/blog/when-your-use-case-half-succeeds-designing-for-partial-failure-in-dotnet

item.title: API Versioning Should Be Your Last Resort
item.description: Most teams reach for v2 too early because they don't have a contract evolution strategy. Here's the API change management approach I prefer: evolve contracts safely, deprecate deliberately, and version only when the old and new worlds can't coexist. Link to article: https://www.milanjovanovic.tech/blog/api-versioning-should-be-your-last-resort
